title: Mildly Flavored Japanese Herring Ochazuke Donabe
description: A delicious and nutritious post-workout Hokkaido-inspired dish featuring jammy herring, crispy toast, and steaming hot donabe broth.

ingredients.name: Gluten-free toast
ingredients.notes: Any brand of gluten-free bread works well.

ingredients.name: Sake rice
ingredients.notes: Short-grain Japanese rice ideal for donabe cooking.

ingredients.name: Dashi granules
ingredients.notes: Available at most Asian grocery stores.

instructions: In a donabe pot, combine 4 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of dashi granules. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and add 2 tablespoons of soy sauce and 2 tablespoons of mirin. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add 1 cup of sake rice to the pot and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the rice is cooked through.
In a separate pan, toast 2 slices of gluten-free bread until crispy and golden brown.
Cut the herring fillets into small pieces and mix with 1 teaspoon of wasabi.
Divide the cooked rice between two bowls and top with the herring mixture, toasted bread, and bamboo shoots.
Serve with a drizzle of ponzu sauce and garnish with green onions and sesame seeds, if using.
